messages.content:
Reading an entire speech from note cards is not an acceptable method of speech delivery.
False
True

False. While it is ideal to deliver a speech without reading from notes, it is still acceptable to use note cards as a reference while delivering a speech. It is important to strike a balance between using notes and maintaining eye contact with the audience.

To determine what language and rhetorical devices would be most effective in a speech, a speaker should
consider:
the purpose and audience of a speech.
what pattern of organization she will use.
the length of the speech.

True. To determine what language and rhetorical devices would be most effective in a speech, a speaker should consider the purpose and audience of the speech. Understanding the audience and their interests and needs will help the speaker select the most effective language and rhetorical devices to connect with them. The pattern of organization and length of the speech are also important factors to consider but do not necessarily determine the effectiveness of language and rhetorical devices used in the speech.

Martin Luther King, Jr. delivered his famous speech:
on television the night that President John F. Kennedy was assassinated.
at the “March on Washington” in 1963.
during his 1968 campaign for president.
right before he was shot and killed in Memphis.
None of the choices are correct.
Martin Luther King, Jr. delivered his famous speech at the “March on Washington” in 1963.

Which of the following sentences is illustrating parallelism?
Sam really enjoys salami sandwiches, to ride his bicycle, and the movies.
The woman is beautiful, smart, and talks too much.
When we get to the mountains we will hike, swim, and rest.
When we get to the mountains we will hike, swim, and rest is illustrating parallelism.

Rhetoric is language or visual images that are designed to persuade or inform an audience.
False
True
True.

To get the attention of the audience, a speaker should:
introduce the speech with an interesting fact or quotation.
politely ask the audience to listen to the speech.
None of the choices are correct.
ask the host to announce his or her presence at the podium.
make the audience laugh.

Introduce the speech with an interesting fact or quotation is the best way to get the attention of the audience. By starting with something interesting or thought-provoking, the speaker is more likely to engage the audience and capture their attention. Politely asking the audience to listen may not be effective, and asking the host to announce the speaker's presence may not be appropriate for all settings. While making the audience laugh can be a great way to engage them, it may not be appropriate for all topics or audiences.

A student that reads fluently has a strong understanding of Greek and Latin roots and recognizes words
with automaticity.
True
False
True.

Abraham Lincoln stated that it was the:
brave men who fought on the battlefield that consecrated the cemetery grounds.
townspeople’s responsibility to consecrate the cemetery.
job of a president to consecrate a cemetery
Abraham Lincoln stated that it was the brave men who fought on the battlefield that consecrated the cemetery grounds.

Abraham Lincoln:
wanted people to continue fighting for the freedoms that the soldiers in
Gettysburg died for.
believed that soldiers who died in Pennsylvania should be buried further North.
stated that Gettysburg was the site of a great Confederate victory.
All of the choices are correct.
thought cemetery grounds should only be consecrated by priests.
Abraham Lincoln wanted people to continue fighting for the freedoms that the soldiers in Gettysburg died for.

Martin Luther King, Jr. helped pass the Thirteenth Amendment to the U.S. Constitution.
False
True
False.

Which of the following sentences is punctuated correctly?
I will tell you one more time: do not touch my computer again.
Lydia’s family is going to Austin Texas for the rodriguez family reunion.
The movie Bambi is my nephew “Carson’s” favorite movie.
I will tell you one more time: do not touch my computer again. is punctuated correctly.
